text.title: A/B Testing Rules and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Statistical and practical significance rules, segment analysis pitfalls, agent limitations, and common mistakes.

rule.statement: Require the agent to assess practical significance (effect size in business terms) in addition to p < 0.05.
rule.rationale: Statistical significance with tiny effect size (e.g., +0.01% conversion) may not justify implementation cost; both tests must pass.

rule.statement: Compute segment results only for pre-specified segments, not post-hoc exploration.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc segment analysis (fishing) inflates false-positive risk via multiple comparisons; pre-specification is the only valid approach.

antipattern.description: Stopping the test early when results look favorable (peeking).
antipattern.reason: Invalidates standard p-values; the full pre-calculated sample is required for valid inference.

antipattern.description: Testing too many things simultaneously in one variant.
antipattern.reason: Multiple simultaneous changes make attribution impossible; you cannot know which change drove the result.

antipattern.description: Ignoring guardrail metrics when declaring a winner.
antipattern.reason: A variant can improve the primary metric while degrading revenue, session depth, or user trust; guardrails prevent shipping net-negative changes.
Undocumented null results cause teams to run the same tests repeatedly; every result has learning value.

rule.statement: Agent "ship" recommendations must be confirmed by a PM or analyst before implementation.
rule.rationale: Agent analysis is advisory; ship decisions require human judgment on business context, timing, and risk appetite.

rule.statement: Agent cannot detect test contamination (users switching between devices) in the data; flag this risk in the test plan.
rule.rationale: Cross-device users who see both control and variant pollute assignment integrity; contamination must be flagged for engineering to address at tracking layer.

rule.statement: Agent may misinterpret segment uplift as a universal win; always check whether the primary metric is significant across the full population.
rule.rationale: One segment winning while the full population is flat is not a ship signal; population-level significance is the primary gate.

text.reference: Python sample size and chi-squared significance calculator
